summary: Completed backend-only regression testing for Android CI workflow linker/LTO mitigation and debug-first artifact policy. Updated and executed pytest checks against workflow/templates; all targeted validations passed.
action_items: Run the GitHub Actions workflow on a branch/PR to validate runtime behavior on actual runner and confirm debug APK artifact upload.
If release still fails intermittently, inspect gradle-build.log for transitive linker flags injected outside generated CMakeLists and extend sanitization scope accordingly.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical defects found in referenced workflow/template files for requested linker/LTO and debug-guard behavior.
Validation here is static regression testing; actual APK installability still requires live GitHub Actions execution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added targeted assertions for: CMakeLists sanitization (-fuse-ld=gold -> lld, remove -flto=thin, disable IPO), debug-first + release best-effort execution order, explicit debug APK guard with optional release handling, and build summary fallback status when only debug APK exists.
rca of the issue: Prior CI failures were driven by release native linker invocation including gold/thin-LTO flags, which require LLVMgold plugin not present on runner. The workflow now sanitizes generated CMakeLists and treats release as non-blocking so installable debug artifact remains guaranteed. No MOCKED APIs used.
